learningObjective: Understand that dragging a point in one direction creates a line segment with one dimension (length). The aha moment: the segment's length depends only on where the point ends up relative to where it started, not on the path taken — dimension measures extent in a direction, not the journey itself.
ahaMoment: Realizing that the segment's length (the dimension created) depends only on where the point ends up relative to where it started, not on the path taken to get there — discovering that dimension measures extent in a direction, not the journey itself

miniSpec.title: Point Budget: Build the Longest Segment
miniSpec.narrative_wrapper: You have exactly 100 movement units to spend. Click to place your starting point, then navigate to reach the farthest destination you can before your budget runs out. The challenge: straight paths consume budget efficiently, while wandering wastes it—but only the straight-line distance from start to finish becomes your final segment.
miniSpec.manipulated_variable: Student clicks directional arrows (up/down/left/right) to move the point in discrete steps, changing the point's cumulative position while depleting a movement budget
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: A dashed potential segment updates in real-time from the start point to the current point position, displaying its measured length; the remaining budget counter decrements with each arrow press and changes color (green→yellow→red) as it depletes
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Three live displays show: (1) total distance traveled (winding path sum), (2) straight-line segment length (start to current), and (3) efficiency ratio between them; a semi-transparent 'wasted movement' zone shows the area between the efficient straight path and the actual wandering route taken
miniSpec.trace_strategy: The complete meandering path taken persists as a thin, semi-transparent trail in a muted color, contrasting sharply with the thick, vibrant straight segment connecting start to current position, making the difference between 'distance traveled' and 'displacement dimension' visually stark
miniSpec.aha_moment: Realizing that the segment's length (the dimension created) depends only on where the point ends up relative to where it started, not on the path taken to get there — discovering that dimension measures extent in a direction, not the journey itself

modelVariables.line1d_startX: X coordinate of the starting point
modelVariables.line1d_startY: Y coordinate of the starting point
modelVariables.line1d_currentX: X coordinate of the current point position
modelVariables.line1d_currentY: Y coordinate of the current point position
modelVariables.line1d_distanceTraveled: Derived: total distance traveled along the winding path
modelVariables.line1d_segmentLength: Derived: straight-line distance from start to current position
modelVariables.line1d_efficiency: Derived: ratio of segment length to distance traveled (0-100%)
modelVariables.line1d_hasStarted: Boolean: whether the game has started (starting point placed)

assertions.description: On load, the game awaits a starting point — budget is full and no segment exists yet
assertions.visualCheck: Canvas shows 'Click to place starting point' instruction, budget shows 100, no segment visible

assertions.description: Each step depletes the budget by the step distance
assertions.visualCheck: Budget counter decreases and path trail extends

assertions.description: Moving in a straight line results in segment length equal to distance traveled
assertions.visualCheck: Path trail and segment overlap perfectly, efficiency shows ~100%

assertions.description: Moving back and forth results in segment length less than distance traveled
assertions.visualCheck: Long winding path visible but short segment; efficiency shows low percentage

assertions.description: Two different paths to the same endpoint produce the same segment length
assertions.visualCheck: Regardless of path taken, segment length remains constant based on endpoint displacement

invariants.description: Straight-line segment length can never exceed the total distance traveled
Efficiency is always between 0 and 100 percent
